finding: The prod-exclusion gate is HOLLOW / vacuous. Its sentinel ZIEE_GALLERY_SEED_MARKER exists ONLY inside a JSDoc comment (support/registry.ts:2 — grep-confirmed it appears nowhere as runtime code, no string literal, no identifier). vite build minifies by default (vite.config.ts sets no build.minify:false; the config comment itself notes Vite 8 uses Rolldown/Oxc minifier by default), and minifiers strip non-legal comments. So if the gallery module ever LEAKED into a prod chunk, the marker (comment-only) would be stripped and the grep would still find nothing -> the check prints 'OK ... absent from all N assets' regardless of a real leak. The gate can never fail. Fix: make the marker a runtime value referenced by real code (e.g. export const GALLERY_SEED_MARKER='ZIEE_GALLERY_SEED_MARKER' consumed by the registry) so tree-shaking either drops it (correct) or bundles it (detectable).

Security manifestation of the hollow-marker bug: this is the only gate guarding that the dev gallery + its mock-API cassette data never ship in the production bundle. Because the sentinel is comment-only and stripped by minification, a genuine leak of dev-only fixtures into dist/ui would pass undetected. Note the gate is also NOT part of `npm run check` (only a standalone `check:gallery-prod-exclusion` requiring --build), so nothing routinely runs even this vacuous check.

isMain guard uses the naive `import.meta.url === `file://${process.argv[1]}``. On Windows this is FALSE (import.meta.url=file:///C:/... vs process.argv[1]=C:\...), and it also breaks on any repo path containing spaces (no URL-encoding). When isMain is false the entire gate body is skipped -> `node gen-overlay-registry.mjs --check` exits 0 with no output = a silently-disabled gate. This branch INTRODUCED the guard here (the script previously ran unconditionally at top level and thus worked on Windows), so it is a Windows regression for check:overlay-registry. The repo already has the portable form (gallery-geometry-audit.mjs:1927 uses pathToFileURL(process.argv[1]).href). Same issue in gen-gallery-seed-registry.mjs:130. (It does match the mirrored gen-override-registry.mjs, which has the same latent bug.)

hasUserSurface can both over- and under-match. Over-match: `path:\s*['"]...` matches ANY object key literally named `path:` (an icon path, file path, config value), not just route paths -> an infra module referencing a non-route `path:` is wrongly flagged as a surface and forced to add a gallery.tsx or allowlist entry. Under-match: only single/double-quoted paths are detected; a route declared with a backtick/template path (path: `/foo/${id}`) is missed -> a real surface escapes the completeness gate. Currently no module trips either (gate is green), so latent.

enumerateModules derives hasSurface from module.tsx ONLY. A module that declares its routes/slots in a separate file imported by module.tsx (e.g. routes.tsx) would present as no-surface and never be required to own a seed -> false-negative undermining the completeness claim. Verified no current module splits routes this way, so latent not active.

parseSeedExceptions accepts a malformed sign-off: the regex only requires the literal `[approved:` with no closing `]` or approver content, so `- NO-SEED: foo — bar [approved:` (empty/unterminated) is accepted as a valid exception. Fail-open in the direction of admitting an under-specified exception. (Conversely a module name glued to the em-dash with no surrounding spaces is silently rejected — fail-safe.)

stripComments line-comment regex `(^|[^:])//[^\n]*` strips from `//` to EOL whenever `//` is not immediately preceded by a colon. This corrupts a string literal containing `//` that is not part of `://` (e.g. `'a//b'`, or a protocol-relative URL `'//cdn'`) -> content after it (which could contain a slot keyword) is dropped, a false-negative. Naive-comment-stripping edge case; low likelihood in a module.tsx.

extractWiredSurfaces matches `surface:\s*'([^']+)'` — SINGLE-quoted only. A per-module gallery.tsx author who writes surface:"modules/foo/Bar" (double quotes) would not be detected as wired -> that host overlay is falsely reported MISSING and fails check:overlay-registry. Relies on biome enforcing single quotes, but biome format is not in `npm run check`. The unit test (TEST-5) only uses single quotes so the gap is uncovered.

index.test.ts is named for the barrel but imports directly from ./hold.ts, never from ./index.ts, so it does not actually exercise the barrel's value re-exports (the file's own docstring concedes tsc covers that). The first assertion (`typeof fn === 'function'`) is a near-hollow smoke check. The other two cases (whenTrue-immediate, holdPatch-applies-N-times) are genuine. Not misleading, just one weak assertion + a misnamed file.

The `if (isMain) {` body (both gen-overlay-registry.mjs and gen-gallery-seed-registry.mjs) is left at column 0, not indented into the block. Harmless functionally and NOT caught by `npm run check` (which only runs biome lint --only=style/noRestrictedImports), but biome format:check / a formatter run would reflag it. Cosmetic drift.

Inconsistent gallery-seed exclusion across the three coverage gates: gen-testid-registry.mjs excludes BOTH gallery.tsx and gallery.ts, but gen-state-matrix.mjs (and gen-gallery-coverage.mjs) exclude only 'gallery.tsx'. The seed contract permits gallery.ts (hasSeed + the runtime glob both accept .ts), so a module using gallery.ts could leak into the state-matrix surface set. coverage.mjs only walks .tsx so it is moot there; state-matrix is the real gap. No gallery.ts files exist today.

walk(DIST) is invoked twice (once to build offenders, once inline in the success console.log to count assets), re-reading the whole dist tree; and the offenders filter reads every JS file fully into memory with readFileSync+includes. Negligible for a one-shot CI script, but the double-walk is avoidable by caching the file list.

Redundant/duplicated cassette key: auth/gallery.tsx spreads ...authCassette (which already seeds 'Auth.getSessionSettings': sessionSettings, added in a prior fix with the explanatory comment in fixtures/auth.ts) AND re-declares 'Auth.getSessionSettings' inline immediately after. The inline literal (updated_at 2026-01-01) silently shadows the authCassette value (updated_at 2026-07-05). Harmless (both type-valid, same TTLs) but dead/duplicate; the inline block + comment can be dropped.

'App.getSetupStatus':{needs_setup:true} is placed into the SINGLE global GALLERY_CASSETTE. The app module's initialize() runs during seedGallery()->loadModules()->initializeModules() and calls checkSetupStatus(), so Stores.App.needsSetup flips to true for the WHOLE gallery session (previously it resolved to undefined via makeSafeEmpty). Currently benign: the SetupRedirect routerEffect is never mounted (pages.tsx renders each surface in an isolated MemoryRouter without the app root's routerEffects slot). Latent coupling — if any future gallery surface mounts the app root / routerEffects, every page would redirect to /setup. Consider scoping the needs-setup seed to the SetupPage surface rather than the global cassette.

The 'renders populated' content assertion `expect(text.length).toBeGreaterThan(40)` reads innerText of the `gallery-page-<slug>` SECTION, which always contains the gallery chrome (Title=page.path + caption 'gallery-page-<slug> · seeded via mock-API'). Measured: chrome alone is >=46 chars for the SHORTEST surface (46 for 'setup', 68-70 for the others) BEFORE any inner page renders. So the threshold is satisfied by chrome even when the seeded component renders nothing (silent seed failure / empty-state). The header comment claims it proves 'more than just the gallery chrome label' and guards 'rendered only chrome' — it does not. The spec's PRIMARY claim (surfaces render POPULATED) is unverified; only the no-crash + no-console-error checks carry weight. Fix: assert on the inner `[data-gallery-frame]` subtree (or a specific role/heading), not the whole section.

Same vacuous content check as gallery-newly-seeded: `expect(text.length).toBeGreaterThan(40)` on the `gallery-page-<slug>` section, whose chrome caption+Title alone is 46-70 chars for all four gap surfaces (setup/settings-sessions/settings-sandbox/files-detail). A regression where a gap cassette (App.getSetupStatus / Auth.getSessionSettings / CodeSandbox.listRootfsVersions / File.get) stops seeding would leave the page empty-but-not-crashed and the test would still pass green — exactly the class of bug this spec exists to catch.

The console-error filter `!/Failed to load resource/i.test(m.text())` (duplicated in gallery-gap-seed:14) drops ALL failed-resource console errors. Combined with the vacuous >40 content check, a seeded surface whose cassette endpoint is missing (unmocked /api → failed request) could pass green with no populated content. Mitigated: it mirrors the runtime-health gate precedent and page-errors (JS exceptions) are still captured. Note only.

SeededSurfaceFrame's AppErrorBoundary uses `fallback={() => null}` (unlike PageFrame/OverlayFrame which use galleryCrashFallback emitting the `gallery-crash` testid). So a crash inside a SEEDED-class surface renders null with NO gallery-crash marker, making any `getByTestId('gallery-crash')).toHaveCount(0)` assertion ineffective for seeded surfaces. NOT triggered by this branch's 3 specs (all four gap slugs + five new slugs resolve as enumerated PAGE routes using galleryCrashFallback, and TEST-11 overlays use OverlayFrame which does emit the marker), but it is a latent gap for any future seeded-surface crash assertion. Pre-existing in the frame, unchanged by the diff.

Forward-merge hazard (not a diff defect): the parallel `main` (sidebar-recent-paging) added 3 seeded surfaces — seeded-recent-convos-loaded/error/loading-more — to the central seededSurfaces.tsx entries region that THIS branch deletes/flips to an aggregator. This branch migrated only the 2 that existed at its base (loading/empty). On merging main, those 3 main-side surfaces must be re-homed into chat/gallery.tsx or coverage is silently lost, and the frozen 155-slug baseline won't flag it. Verified: all 155 merge-base slugs migrated with zero drops; the 3 are genuinely main-only.

TEST-17's 'shared module via cross-workspace cassette' test targets slug settings-users, but that page is populated by User.list, which is present in the desktop crawl base (fixtures/crawl.generated.ts). The web user/gallery.tsx contributes only `overlays` (no `cassette`), so SHARED_CASSETTE seeds NOTHING for that page. The assertion (>40 chars, no errors) passes identically from the crawl base alone, even if module-seed.ts's `...SHARED_CASSETTE` spread were deleted or the cross-workspace `@/dev/gallery/support/registry` import were broken. The test therefore does not verify the cross-workspace cassette inheritance it claims to prove. A faithful target is a shared surface whose data comes ONLY from a web module `cassette` key absent from crawl (e.g. scheduler ScheduledTask.list, notification Notification.unreadCount, knowledge-base, voice).

module-seed.ts exports OVERLAY_ENTRIES (SHARED_OVERLAYS + desktop overlays) but nothing consumes it: fixtures/index.ts imports only MODULE_CASSETTE, and desktop overlays.tsx defines its OWN hardcoded `OVERLAY_ENTRIES = []` with a DIFFERENT OverlayEntry shape (render/label vs the web component/title/open). The web overlays.tsx re-exports registry's OVERLAY_ENTRIES and pages/overlays render them; the desktop gallery does not. So the desktop gallery inherits ONLY the shared cassette, never shared web overlay/seeded/deepState surfaces — the 'inherits the web seed for free' claim is cassette-only. Dead export; also a parity gap if overlay coverage on desktop is intended (desktop overlays.tsx documents a deliberate page-focus, so likely intended, but the export is misleading).

Slug uniqueness is enforced per-set only: assertUniqueSlugs(DESKTOP_GALLERIES) checks the desktop-only set; the web registry checks its own DISCOVERED set separately. There is NO cross-set (shared-web vs desktop-only) slug-collision guard, so a desktop-only overlay/deep/seeded slug that duplicates a shared web slug would NOT be caught, silently producing duplicate slugs in the merged OVERLAY_ENTRIES — violating registry-core's stated contract ('Slugs must be unique across overlays/deep/seeded'). Current blast radius is low because desktop does not enumerate seeded/overlay/deep surfaces (only the cassette), but it is a latent invariant break the moment desktop wires those surfaces.

SHARED_CASSETTE (web-typed Cassette, keyed by the WEB ApiEndpoint union + web response types) is spread into the desktop Cassette via `as Cassette`. The cast suppresses any compile-time check that a shared endpoint's response SHAPE matches the desktop api-client type. Practically safe: shared surfaces render the same web components (via the @/ fallback) against web-shaped data, and a web-only endpoint key is simply never matched by the desktop mockApi (harmless extra key). No runtime defect found, but there is no guard against a genuine web-vs-desktop endpoint divergence.

Not introduced by this commit and out of its stated scope ('both new gate scripts'), but noting: gen-override-registry.mjs:188 and seam-codemod.mjs:202 still use the naive `file://${process.argv[1]}` form and would be silently disabled on Windows. Pre-existing; no regression from this commit.

Harmless. No gallery.ts files exist in the repo, so the added `e !== 'gallery.ts'` clauses (gen-gallery-coverage x2, gen-state-matrix) are currently no-ops (future-proofing). In gen-gallery-coverage the clause is additionally dead because the same predicate already requires /\.tsx$/ (gallery.ts can never reach it). Not a defect — no behavior change and cannot exclude a real surface.

All 5 seeded-recent-convos-* entries (loading/empty/loaded/error/loading-more) use the correct recent* ChatHistory fields (recentInitialized/recentLoading/recentLoadingMore/recentConversations/recentTotal/recentHasMore/recentPage/recentError), matching what RecentConversationsWidget.tsx reads. Main SPLIT the store (added a parallel recent* set) rather than renaming — the legacy isInitialized/loading/conversations/error fields still exist and drive ChatHistoryPage. seeded-chat-history-list correctly uses those legacy fields; seeded-message-list-empty reads Chat-store fields (messages/loading/isStreaming), unaffected. No stale field name found.

Main's 5 new entries (seeded-recent-convos-loaded/error/loading-more, seeded-conversation-list-long{,-narrow}) re-homed verbatim: slug/title/note/component/setup byte-identical to origin/main seededSurfaces.tsx (only indentation shifted for nesting). The ./ConversationListLongDemo import was correctly rewritten to @/dev/gallery/ConversationListLongDemo (file exists at src-app/ui/src/dev/gallery/ConversationListLongDemo.tsx, named export ConversationListLongDemo). mkRecentConvos helper present at lines 61-71 and byte-identical to main's. All support imports (holdForever/holdPatch/lazyNamed/lazyProps/whenTrue) resolve via the support barrel.

The 3 branch-wired drawers (ScheduledTaskFormDrawer, KnowledgeBaseFormDrawer, UploadModelDrawer) are correctly REMOVED from the allowlist. Main's ScheduledTaskCard (inline confirm) is correctly KEPT (now the last entry of the hosts section, valid JSON, no trailing comma). JSON parses clean. No dropped or duplicated entries.

Full-tree slug diff (origin/main vs HEAD across all ui/src *.ts[x]): all 160 main gallery slugs are present on HEAD — ZERO lost. HEAD adds only the 3 branch-wired overlay slugs (overlay-{scheduled-task,knowledge-base}-form-drawer, overlay-upload-model-drawer) plus d1/s1/o1/dup which are registry.test.ts fixtures. Each re-homed slug is defined in exactly one file (chat/gallery.tsx); no duplicate/colliding slug definitions across modules (the only duplicate 'dup' line is the deliberate collision-detection unit test).
